title: Complex punctuation prose
description: Mixed punctuation — semicolons, dashes, parentheses at speed
text: The proposal had three goals: reduce costs (by 15%), improve reliability — measured by uptime — and grow the user base; together, these would set the company up for a strong second half. The plan was bold but realistic.

tips.text: Semicolons, dashes, parentheses: the marks that separate plain writing from polished writing. This is the editor's toolkit.
Each mark keeps its own beat. A semicolon is a clean break; a dash opens a wider pause; the parenthesis tucks an aside out of the way.

tips.target: The proposal had three goals: reduce costs (by 15%), improve reliability — measured by uptime — and grow the

tips.text: Don't agonize over each mark as you go. Type the line, then read it back for sense. The hands learn faster than the second-guessing mind.

tips.target: user base; together, these would set the company up for a strong second half. The plan was bold but realistic.

character_tips.anna: Punctuation like this is what marks the beginner from the hand who knows the craft. Drill it until it is automatic. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: A serious report uses every one of these marks. Master them and business writing holds no surprises. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: A whole crowd of marks in one line. Take them one at a time, keep the pace even, and none will trip you. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: This is writing with its good clothes on. Set the marks down with care and the sentence carries real weight. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
